step_by_step_thinking:
Based on the detailed discussion provided, we understand that CD8+ T cells (also known as CTLs), specifically identified as "Cd8" from the options, are indeed the key players in directly killing infected host cells due to their cytotoxic capabilities. My agreement stems from recognizing the specificity of each T-cell subset and their unique functional roles within the immune system. Thus, maintaining consistency with the prior reasoning, my position strongly supports the selection of Option C ("Cd8").

Final Answer Choice: C To clarify any potential confusion regarding nomenclature used previously versus standardization across platforms like NCBI databases, both "Cd8" and its more commonly recognized form "CD8" refer to the same antigen receptor expressed on certain subsets of T cells. However, for uniformity and alignment with typical biomedical terminology, "CD8" would generally be preferred even though either spelling fits contextually well here. Nonetheless, considering format requirements strictly necessitated using lowercase “cd” per instruction guidelines exactly matching available choices listed originally.
